worked: **What didn't work / gotchas:** (1) The migration trap — `rsync -a` preserves modes but `git clone/pull` restores *committed* modes, so a never-committed `chmod +x` survives a file-copy but dies on the next clone. Git tracks only the exec bit; stage the mode change (`git add` after chmod, or `git update-index --chmod=+x`). (2) Substring `permissions.deny` rules (`Bash(* /dev*)`) are read-blocking tyranny — they fired on my own grep, commit message, and IPC reply. (3) `permissions.deny` is cached at session-start while PreToolUse **hook scripts are re-read per call** — so a removed deny rule lingers this session but a newly-registered hook is live immediately. (4) Heredoc-composed JSON/markdown injects whitespace/newlines → use the Write tool for structured fixtures (bit me on the visual JSON; same stub-fixture trap that gave mistakes-infra a false bug).

**User feedback / preferences:** A "too noisy/aggressive" complaint = tune down, NOT turn off (request is a ceiling). Reassurance ("I trust claude") ≠ removal mandate. Don't blind-`reset --hard`; do a deliberate, lossless branch merge. Per-session, server-independent, simplest-thing-that-works for auto-continue. Always values honesty about non-involvement over capitulation.

**Notes for future agents:** New-machine git is reconciled (`main`=`origin/main`=`8fbf134`, ahead 0/behind 0). Auto-continue is opt-in per session via `auto-continue.sh on [max_retries] [cooldown]` — inert otherwise. The system-dir guard mutes via `touch ~/.claude/.allow-system-writes`; the cred guard via `.allow-cred-write`.

pending: (User-side, empirical) When a real rate-limit next hits a flagged session, check `~/.claude/auto-continue/<sid8>.log` to confirm CC emits the `Stop` event on error-exhaustion — the one link untestable synthetically.

(Open, deferred) Two systemic IPC gaps surfaced but NOT fixed (unprompted): no presence-heartbeat (busy session looks "offline" to peers); bug-handoff-by-mailbox assumes one owner per alias (two "audit"-ish sessions collided).
(Carried, not this session) The `CLAUDE.md` atone-section + `settings.json` deny-list were edited by the user/linter mid-session — left untouched intentionally.
